briefing.headline: Cover water — spawn muddy conditions reward moving baits that trigger reaction strikes.
briefing.gameplan:
In spawn with muddy water and stable conditions, moving baits presentations shine.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better act
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...

Wheeler: The 7'1" MH casting rod is great for jigs, spinnerbaits, and single-hook baits around tight cover like docks, laydowns, and overhangs; the 7'4" MH moderate-fast casting rod is great for offshore cranking and big swimbaits with its parabol...

Best for pitching to isolated cover - docks, logs, laydowns, boulders, stumps. A little tough to sink into thick cover. Effective on both smallmouth and largemouth, spawning, cruising, and fry guarding fish

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
